title: Cross-reference closure graph, per-rule closure manifests, and completeness/self-check harness (incl. ZR 11-25 suffix inheritance)
objective: PROPOSED — NOT CONTRACTED (owner approval required to move backlog -> ready). Build a deterministic directed graph over the ingested corpus covering: linked AND unlinked textual section citations; definitions imported from §12-10 and other scoped definition sections; §11-25 suffix inheritance; 'except as otherwise provided' relationships; general-to-special priority; exceptions/modifications; special-purpose district overrides; appendices/mapped areas; tables and footnotes; exception language; amendment/supersession links; and citation-like text that could not be parsed (amendment item 8). Every releasable rule family gets a closure manifest: entry sections; all reachable controlling sections; imported definitions; applicability predicates; exceptions; overrides; UNRESOLVED reference/citation candidates; required property facts; source version/hash per node. A rule may proceed ONLY if all reachable references in its declared scope are resolved; otherwise a typed unsupported/professional_review_required result. Unresolved citation candidates MUST appear in the closure manifest and BLOCK a final rule value. An unresolved reference is NEVER treated as non-applicable; searching one chapter cannot satisfy an absence assertion. AI-extracted edges remain PROPOSED until deterministic evidence or required human review validates them. Cycles terminate deterministically; renumbered/deleted sections are detected. Full spec: project-control/reports/M3-CORPUS-REPLAN-PROPOSAL.md.
business_reason: Completeness must be proven by reachable-reference closure over linked AND unlinked/table/footnote citations, not assembled manually from one chapter; §11-25 suffix inheritance and §23-422's express suffix exclusion must be modeled before any bare-R5 applicability or absence claim, and before the downstream M4 yard/coverage slices are re-scoped. This is the structural precondition the owner set for regenerating the coverage/rear/front/side slice boundaries.
inputs: M3-T002 accepted AS PRODUCTION CORPUS (durable content-addressed captures + fidelity evidence; NOT a fixture-only engineering increment): per-section raw+normalized hashes, cross-reference hrefs, definitions, last-amended/effective dates, amendment/supersession records, tables/footnotes — the graph's node/edge source
M3-T002 exported ingest/parser/fidelity core (services/api/app/corpus/ingest|parsers|fidelity) — IMPORT read-only; do not mutate
M3-T001 accepted: LEGAL_CORPUS_COVERAGE_MATRIX.md (domains a manifest maps to) + legal_source_manifest schema (per-node version/hash record) + authoritative status vocabulary

ZR §11-25 (a district designation's regulations apply to a suffixed designation unless the Resolution expressly provides separate provisions); §23-422 express exclusion 'except R5 Districts with a letter suffix'; §12-10 definitions

services/api/app/rules/** (M4-T001 engine): RuleResult coverage vocabulary, rule_conflict/competing-rules trace shape, unsupported/professional_review_required outcomes the closure result must produce — consume unchanged
outputs: services/api/app/corpus/closure/** — graph builder, linked+unlinked+table/footnote citation extractor, §11-25 suffix-inheritance resolver, closure-manifest generator, reachability + deterministic cycle termination, renumber/delete detector, proposed-vs-validated edge state

packages/contracts/schemas/v1/closure_manifest.schema.json — NEW versioned internal manifest schema (additive; deterministic version + $id; entry sections, reachable sections, imported definitions, applicability predicates, exceptions, overrides, unresolved candidates, required facts, per-node version/hash)

packages/contracts/schemas/v1/fixtures/closure_manifest/{positive,negative}/*.json — executable schema-validation fixtures (positive validates; negatives missing unresolved-reference field or per-node source-version/hash fail)
services/api/tests/corpus/closure/** — deterministic cross-reference-closure harness incl. adversarial unlinked-reference + table-footnote 'cannot be silently missed' tests

services/api/tests/fixtures/corpus/closure/** — bounded frozen fixtures: §23-361/R5B inheritance, §23-422 suffix exclusion, cyclic ref, renumbered/deleted section, unresolved definition, unlinked textual citation, table footnote citation
allowed_paths: packages/contracts/schemas/v1/fixtures/closure_manifest/** (positive + negative closure-manifest fixtures for AS-10 executable validation)
forbidden_paths: services/api/app/corpus/ingest/**, parsers/**, fidelity/**, versioning/** (owned by M3-T002 — import read-only, never mutate), services/api/app/corpus/construction_code/** and overlay/** (owned by M3-T004)
Editing the rules evaluator core or any ruleset (consume the engine unchanged; this task produces closure inputs, not rules)

Publishing/Verifying any rule; emitting a final value when any reachable reference/citation candidate is unresolved; treating an unresolved reference as non-applicable; treating an AI-extracted edge as validated without deterministic evidence or human review
Asserting 'confirmed absent' for any modifier; asserting completeness from a single-chapter scan

Editing canonical contracts (property_profile/rule_evaluation/coverage_status/scenario); apps/web/**; project-control/** except own reports; .claude/** except own agent-memory; adding any new runtime dependency (stdlib graph)
acceptance_scenarios: AS-1 suffix inheritance: §23-361 applied to R5B imports §11-25 inheritance unless the section expressly provides a different suffix rule -> R5B inherits R5 provisions where no separate R5B provision exists.
AS-2 express exclusion wins: §23-422's express 'except R5 Districts with a letter suffix' prevents R5 values leaking to R5A/R5B/R5D; the closure records the express exclusion, not inheritance.

AS-3 unresolved blocks value: an unresolved definition/exception/citation candidate in a rule's reachable set produces NO final value — a typed unsupported/professional_review_required result instead.
AS-4 cycle termination: a reference cycle terminates deterministically (no infinite loop, no arbitrary cutoff that hides a node).
AS-5 renumber/delete detection: a renumbered or deleted section referenced by the graph is detected and flagged unresolved, not silently followed to a stale node.

AS-6 manifest completeness: each releasable rule family's closure manifest lists entry sections, all reachable controlling sections, imported definitions, applicability predicates, exceptions, overrides, unresolved candidates, required property facts, and source version/hash per node.
AS-7 determinism: identical corpus version -> byte-identical closure manifest + graph.

AS-8 unlinked + table/footnote citations (item 8): closure processes linked AND unlinked textual citations, definition imports, tables/footnotes, exception language, amendment links, and citation-like text that could not be parsed; adversarial tests prove an unlinked reference or a table footnote citation CANNOT be silently missed.

AS-9 proposed-edge discipline (item 8): an AI-extracted edge is recorded as PROPOSED and cannot contribute to a resolved closure (or a final rule value) until deterministic evidence or required human review validates it.

AS-10 closure_manifest schema validation (executable): (a) closure_manifest.schema.json itself passes JSON Schema meta-validation; (b) a complete positive closure-manifest fixture validates; (c) negative fixtures FAIL — including one missing the unresolved-reference/citation-candidate field and one missing per-node source-version/hash; (d) the schema's version and $id are deterministic (byte-identical across regeneration); (e) it stays an internal additive schema, not promoted to a canonical cross-tier contract without a justified consumer.
NC-1 single-chapter absence: a search restricted to one chapter cannot satisfy an absence assertion; the harness asserts such an assertion is rejected as unsupported.
NC-2 unresolved != non-applicable: a reference/citation the graph cannot resolve is never coerced to 'not applicable' or a default value.

NC-3 bare-R5 not auto-ambiguous: bare R5 in a section with no separate suffix provisions is modeled via §11-25 inheritance (not flagged inherently ambiguous), while a section that expressly lists distinct R5A/R5B/R5D provisions lets those express provisions control.
risks: PROPOSAL ONLY: no dispatch until owner approves the packet + dependency order.

DEPENDS ON PRODUCTION M3-T002: this task requires M3-T002 accepted as the durable production corpus (not a fixture-only increment); a fixture-only M3-T002 does NOT satisfy this dependency (amendment item 3).
GATES M4: M4-T007 and every later yard/coverage/rear/front/side rule DEPENDS ON accepted M3-T003; the slice boundaries are regenerated from this closure set, not from a hand-assembled chapter list.

SILENT-MISS HAZARD: unlinked textual citations, table footnotes, and unparseable citation-like text are the highest-risk silent gaps — they must surface as unresolved candidates that block a final value, never be dropped.
PROPOSED-EDGE DISCIPLINE: AI-extracted edges are proposed until validated; a proposed edge must not silently complete a closure.
LEGAL BOUNDARY: closure resolves references structurally; where resolution requires legal interpretation, the result is professional_review_required, never a guessed edge.
requirement_refs: project-control/reports/M3-CORPUS-REPLAN-PROPOSAL.md — full spec (owner directive 2026-07-23 + amendment items 1,8; §11-25 correction)
harness_assignments: CROSS-REFERENCE-CLOSURE HARNESS (owner directive section 6 + amendment item 8): §23-361/R5B imports §11-25 unless expressly overridden; §23-422 express suffix exclusion prevents R5->R5A/R5B/R5D leakage; unresolved definition/exception/citation-candidate produces no final value; cycles terminate deterministically; renumbered/deleted sections detected; single-chapter search cannot satisfy an absence assertion; unlinked references + table footnotes cannot be silently missed; AI-extracted edges are proposed until validated.

LOT/APPLICABILITY + RULE-BEHAVIOR + ARCHITECT-BENCHMARK harnesses (owner directive section 6) are ASSIGNED DOWNSTREAM to the M4 yard/coverage slices that consume this closure — M3-T003 provides the applicability-predicate + suffix-inheritance foundation they assert against; it does not itself classify lots or evaluate rules.
